documentation.value:
Specifies the display behavior of an element.

Values: `block`, `inline`, `inline-block`, `flex`, `grid`, `none`, `contents`, `inline-flex`, `inline-grid`, `flow-root`, `table`, `list-item`.

Sets how an element is positioned in a document. Works with `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, and `z-index`.

Values: `static`, `relative`, `absolute`, `fixed`, `sticky`.

Shorthand for `flex-grow`, `flex-shrink`, and `flex-basis`.

Syntax: `flex: <grow> <shrink> <basis>;`

Examples:
- `flex: 1;` — grow to fill space
- `flex: 0 0 auto;` — don't grow or shrink
- `flex: 1 1 0%;` — distribute space evenly

Defines the columns of a grid container.

Examples:
- `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;`
- `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);`
- `grid-template-columns: minmax(200px, 1fr) 3fr;`
- `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto;`

Defines the rows of a grid container.

Examples:
- `grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;`
- `grid-template-rows: repeat(3, minmax(100px, auto));`

Sets the margin area on all four sides.

Syntax: `margin: <top> <right> <bottom> <left>;`

Shorthand patterns:
- `margin: 10px;` — all sides
- `margin: 10px 20px;` — vertical | horizontal
- `margin: 10px 20px 30px;` — top | horizontal | bottom
- `margin: 10px 20px 30px 40px;` — top | right | bottom | left
- `margin: 0 auto;` — center horizontally

Sets the padding area on all four sides.

Syntax: `padding: <top> <right> <bottom> <left>;`

Accepts length, percentage, or `0`. Does not accept negative values or `auto`.

Sets the foreground color of text and text decorations.

Accepts: named colors, hex (`#fff`), `rgb()`, `rgba()`, `hsl()`, `hsla()`, `oklch()`, `lab()`, `lch()`, `color()`, `currentColor`, `inherit`, `transparent`.

Shorthand for `background-color`, `background-image`, `background-position`, `background-size`, `background-repeat`, `background-origin`, `background-clip`, and `background-attachment`.

Examples:
- `background: #fff;`
- `background: url('bg.png') no-repeat center/cover;`
- `background: linear-gradient(to right, #000, #fff);`

Shorthand for `border-width`, `border-style`, and `border-color`.

Syntax: `border: <width> <style> <color>;`

Styles: `none`, `solid`, `dashed`, `dotted`, `double`, `groove`, `ridge`, `inset`, `outset`.

Shorthand for `font-style`, `font-variant`, `font-weight`, `font-size`/`line-height`, and `font-family`.

Syntax: `font: [style] [variant] [weight] size[/line-height] family;`

Example: `font: italic bold 16px/1.5 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;`

Shorthand for `animation-name`, `animation-duration`, `animation-timing-function`, `animation-delay`, `animation-iteration-count`, `animation-direction`, `animation-fill-mode`, and `animation-play-state`.

Syntax: `animation: name duration timing-function delay iteration-count direction fill-mode;`

Example: `animation: fadeIn 0.3s ease-in-out forwards;`

Shorthand for `transition-property`, `transition-duration`, `transition-timing-function`, and `transition-delay`.

Syntax: `transition: property duration timing-function delay;`

Examples:
- `transition: all 0.3s ease;`
- `transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.3s ease-out;`

Applies a 2D or 3D transformation to an element.

Functions: `translate()`, `translateX()`, `translateY()`, `rotate()`, `scale()`, `scaleX()`, `scaleY()`, `skew()`, `skewX()`, `skewY()`, `matrix()`, `perspective()`, `translate3d()`, `rotate3d()`, `scale3d()`.

Sets the z-order of a positioned element and its descendants. Only works on elements with `position` other than `static`.

Values: integer (positive or negative), `auto`.

Sets what to do when content overflows the element's box.

Values: `visible`, `hidden`, `clip`, `scroll`, `auto`.

Also: `overflow-x`, `overflow-y` for individual axis control.

Sets the type of mouse cursor when hovering over an element.

Common values: `pointer`, `default`, `text`, `move`, `not-allowed`, `grab`, `grabbing`, `crosshair`, `wait`, `progress`, `help`, `zoom-in`, `zoom-out`, `none`, `url()` for custom cursors.

Sets the opacity level for an element. Affects the entire element including contents.

Range: `0` (fully transparent) to `1` (fully opaque).

Note: Unlike `visibility: hidden`, opacity `0` still occupies space and receives events.

Defines how the width and height of an element are calculated.

- `content-box` (default): Width/height only include content
- `border-box`: Width/height include padding and border

Best practice: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }`

Matches when the user interacts with an element via a pointing device (mouse hover).

Specificity: adds (0, 1, 0) to the selector.

Example: `a:hover { color: blue; text-decoration: underline; }`

Matches when an element has received focus (keyboard tab, click, or programmatic focus).

Best practice: Always provide visible `:focus` styles for accessibility. Consider `:focus-visible` for keyboard-only focus.

Creates a pseudo-element as the last child of the selected element. Requires `content` property.

Commonly used for decorative elements, clearfixes, and overlays.

Matches elements based on their position among siblings.

Syntax: `:nth-child(An+B [of S]?)`

Examples:
- `:nth-child(2)` — second child
- `:nth-child(odd)` / `:nth-child(2n+1)` — odd children
- `:nth-child(even)` / `:nth-child(2n)` — even children
- `:nth-child(3n)` — every third child
- `:nth-child(-n+3)` — first three children

Matches the first child element. Equivalent to `:nth-child(1)`.

Example: `li:first-child { font-weight: bold; }`

Applies styles conditionally based on media queries.

Media types: `all`, `screen`, `print`
Media features: `width`, `height`, `aspect-ratio`, `orientation`, `resolution`, `prefers-color-scheme`, `prefers-reduced-motion`, `hover`, `pointer`.

Logical operators: `and`, `not`, `only`, `,` (or).

Imports a stylesheet into the current stylesheet. Must appear before all other rules except `@charset` and `@layer`.

Syntax:
- `@import url('file.css');`
- `@import url('print.css') print;`
- `@import url('mobile.css') screen and (max-width: 768px);`
- `@import 'file.css' layer(name);`

completions.label: @font-face

documentation.value:
Defines a custom font to be used in the document.

Descriptors: `font-family`, `src`, `font-weight`, `font-style`, `font-display`, `unicode-range`, `font-stretch`, `font-variant`, `font-feature-settings`.

`font-display` values: `auto`, `block`, `swap`, `fallback`, `optional`.

Feature query — applies styles only if the browser supports a given CSS feature.

Examples:
- `@supports (display: grid) { ... }`
- `@supports not (gap: 1rem) { ... }`
- `@supports (display: grid) and (gap: 1rem) { ... }`
- `@supports selector(:has()) { ... }`

Declares cascade layers to control the order of specificity.

Syntax:
- `@layer name;` — declare layer order
- `@layer name { ... }` — assign rules to layer
- `@layer name1, name2, name3;` — define layer order
- `@import url('lib.css') layer(lib);`

Layers declared earlier have lower priority.

Inserts the value of a CSS custom property.

Syntax: `var(--property-name, fallback-value)`

Example:
```css
:root { --primary: #3490dc; }
.btn { color: var(--primary, blue); }
```
Fallback can itself use `var()`: `var(--a, var(--b, #000))`.

completions.label: Flexbox Center

documentation.value:
Centers child elements both horizontally and vertically using Flexbox.

This is the most common centering technique in modern CSS.

completions.label: Grid Layout

documentation.value: Creates a responsive grid layout using `auto-fill` and `minmax()` for fluid column sizing without media queries.

Mobile-first media query for tablet and above (768px). Common breakpoints:
- 480px: Mobile landscape
- 768px: Tablet
- 1024px: Desktop
- 1280px: Large desktop

Defines a CSS custom property (CSS variable) on the `:root` pseudo-class for global usage.

Custom properties are inherited and can be overridden in nested scopes.
Uses the `prefers-color-scheme` media feature to apply dark mode styles based on user system preference.

Performs calculations to determine CSS property values. Supports `+`, `-`, `*`, `/` and can mix units.

Examples:
- `width: calc(100% - 2rem);`
- `font-size: calc(1rem + 0.5vw);`
- `height: calc(100vh - var(--header-height));`

Clamps a value between a minimum and maximum.

Syntax: `clamp(MIN, VAL, MAX)`

Example: `font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem);` — responsive font that never goes below 1rem or above 2rem.

Sets the gap (gutter) between rows and columns in flex and grid layouts.

Shorthand for `row-gap` and `column-gap`.

- `gap: 1rem;` — uniform gap
- `gap: 1rem 2rem;` — row gap | column gap

completions.label: Visually Hidden

documentation.value: Hides an element visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Preferred over `display: none` for accessibility.

Sets the width of an element's content area.

Values: `auto`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`, `min-content`, `max-content`, `fit-content()`.

Sets the height of an element's content area.

Values: `auto`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`, `min-content`, `max-content`, `fit-content()`, `100vh`, `100dvh`.
Sets the minimum width of an element. Overrides `width` and `max-width` when they result in a smaller value.
Sets the maximum width of an element. Commonly used for centered layouts: `max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto;`.
Sets the minimum height. Common pattern: `min-height: 100vh;` for full-page layouts.
Sets the maximum height. Commonly used with `overflow: auto` for scrollable regions.
Specifies the vertical position of a positioned element from the top edge.
Specifies the horizontal position from the right edge of a positioned element's containing block.
Specifies the vertical position from the bottom edge of a positioned element's containing block.
Specifies the horizontal position from the left edge of a positioned element's containing block.

Shorthand for `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`.

`inset: 0;` fills the containing block completely.

Defines the main axis direction for a flex container.

Values: `row`, `row-reverse`, `column`, `column-reverse`.

Controls whether flex items wrap onto multiple lines.

Values: `nowrap`, `wrap`, `wrap-reverse`.

Distributes space along the main axis.

Values: `flex-start`, `flex-end`, `center`, `space-between`, `space-around`, `space-evenly`.

Aligns flex/grid items along the cross axis.

Values: `stretch`, `flex-start`, `flex-end`, `center`, `baseline`.
Distributes space between flex/grid lines on the cross axis. Only applies with multiple lines.
Overrides `align-items` for this specific flex/grid item on the cross axis.
Specifies how much a flex item should grow relative to siblings. Default: `0`.
Specifies how much a flex item should shrink. Default: `1`. Set `0` to prevent shrinking.
Sets the initial main size of a flex item before space distribution.
Sets the visual order of a flex/grid item. Default: `0`. Negative values move earlier.

Shorthand for `grid-row-start` / `grid-column-start` / `grid-row-end` / `grid-column-end`.

Also used with `grid-template-areas` for named placement.

Defines named grid areas using ASCII art syntax.

Example:
```css
grid-template-areas:
  'header header'
  'sidebar main'
  'footer footer';
```

Controls how auto-placed items flow into the grid.

Values: `row`, `column`, `dense`, `row dense`, `column dense`.
Shorthand for `align-items` + `justify-items`. `place-items: center;` centers grid items.
Shorthand for `align-content` + `justify-content`.

Sets the size of the font.

Units: `px`, `em`, `rem`, `%`, `vw`. Fluid: `clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem)`.

Sets the weight (boldness) of the font.

100=Thin, 300=Light, 400=Normal, 500=Medium, 700=Bold, 900=Black.

Sets the font family. Always end with a generic fallback.

Generics: `serif`, `sans-serif`, `monospace`, `system-ui`.
Sets the line height. Unitless values (1.5) are recommended as they scale with font-size. WCAG recommends 1.5+ for body text.
Sets spacing between characters. Negative values tighten text. Useful for headings (-0.025em) or uppercase text (0.05em).

Aligns inline content within a block.

Values: `left`, `right`, `center`, `justify`, `start`, `end`.

Shorthand for text decoration line, style, color, and thickness.

Lines: `none`, `underline`, `overline`, `line-through`.
Styles: `solid`, `double`, `dotted`, `dashed`, `wavy`.

Controls capitalization of text.

Values: `none`, `capitalize`, `uppercase`, `lowercase`.

Specifies how overflowed text is signaled. Values: `clip`, `ellipsis`.

Requires `overflow: hidden` and `white-space: nowrap`.

Controls how whitespace and newlines are handled.

Values: `normal`, `nowrap`, `pre`, `pre-wrap`, `pre-line`, `break-spaces`.

Controls line breaking rules within words.

Values: `normal`, `break-all`, `keep-all`, `break-word`.

Allows unbreakable strings to be broken to prevent overflow.

Values: `normal`, `break-word`, `anywhere`.
Sets the background color of an element. Supports all CSS color formats including alpha.
Sets one or more background images. Supports URLs and gradient functions.

Specifies the size of background images.

Values: `auto`, `cover`, `contain`, `<length> <length>`.

Sets the initial position of a background image.

Keywords: `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, `center`. Accepts `<position>` pairs.

Sets how background images are repeated.

Values: `repeat`, `no-repeat`, `repeat-x`, `repeat-y`, `space`, `round`.

Rounds the corners of an element.

`border-radius: 50%;` creates a circle. 1-4 values: TL, TR, BR, BL.

Adds shadow effects around an element.

Syntax: `[inset] <x> <y> <blur> [spread] <color>`. Supports multiple shadows.

Draws a line outside the element's border. Does NOT affect layout.

Syntax: `<width> <style> <color>`.
Applies graphical effects. Functions: `blur()`, `brightness()`, `contrast()`, `grayscale()`, `hue-rotate()`, `invert()`, `opacity()`, `saturate()`, `sepia()`, `drop-shadow()`.

Applies filter effects to the area behind an element. Requires transparent/semi-transparent background.

Glass morphism: `backdrop-filter: blur(10px);`
Sets how replaced elements (``, `
Sets a preferred aspect ratio. Examples: `16 / 9`, `1` (square), `4 / 3`.
Sets element visibility. `hidden` hides element but preserves layout space (unlike `display: none`).
Controls whether an element responds to pointer events. `none` makes clicks pass through.
Controls whether text can be selected. `none` prevents selection, `all` selects entire element on click.
Sets whether an element is user-resizable. Requires `overflow` other than `visible`.
Sets whether scrolling is instant or smooth. `html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }` for smooth anchor links.
Enables scroll snapping. Values: `x mandatory`, `y mandatory`, `x proximity`, `y proximity`.
Hints to the browser about upcoming changes for optimization. Use sparingly.

Indicates containment restrictions for performance.

Values: `none`, `strict`, `content`, `size`, `layout`, `style`, `paint`.

Establishes element as a containment context for container queries.

Values: `normal`, `inline-size`, `size`.
Names a container for targeted `@container` queries.
Sets the accent color for checkboxes, radios, range sliders, and progress bars.
Indicates supported color schemes. `light dark` enables native dark mode for form controls and scrollbars.

Defines how element content blends with content behind it.

Values: `normal`, `multiply`, `screen`, `overlay`, `darken`, `lighten`, `color-dodge`, etc.
Creates a new stacking context. `isolate` prevents `mix-blend-mode` from affecting ancestors.
Returns the smallest value from a list. Example: `width: min(100%, 600px);`.
Returns the largest value. Example: `width: max(300px, 50%);`.
Matches the last child element. Equivalent to `:nth-last-child(1)`.
Matches when element has focus AND the browser determines focus should be visible (typically keyboard focus). Preferred over `:focus` for custom focus indicators.
Matches when the element or any of its descendants has focus. Useful for highlighting parent containers of focused inputs.

The parent selector — matches if any descendant matches the argument.

Examples: `.card:has(img)`, `.form:has(:invalid)`, `h2:has(+ p)`.

Matches any element matched by one of the selectors in the list. Simplifies complex selectors.

Specificity: Takes the highest specificity selector. Use `:where()` for zero specificity.
Like `:is()` but with zero specificity. Ideal for default styles that should be easily overridable.

Matches elements that do NOT match the given selector.

Example: `p:not(.special)`, `input:not(:disabled)`.
Styles the marker box of a list item (bullet or number). Properties allowed: color, font, content, and a few others.
Styles the portion of text selected by the user. Limited properties: `color`, `background-color`, `text-decoration`, `text-shadow`.
Styles the placeholder text of input and textarea elements.

Registers a custom property with a type, initial value, and inheritance.

Enables animation of custom properties and type checking.

Scopes styles to a subtree of the DOM. Limits style applicability without increasing specificity.

Syntax: `@scope (.parent) to (.limit) { }`.

completions.label: Truncate Text

documentation.value: Truncates overflowing text with an ellipsis. The classic three-property pattern.
Truncates text after N lines using `-webkit-line-clamp`. Works in all modern browsers despite the `-webkit-` prefix.
Creates a sticky header that sticks to the top when scrolling past it.

completions.label: Absolute Center

documentation.value: Centers an element absolutely using `translate(-50%, -50%)`.
The shortest CSS centering technique: `display: grid; place-items: center;`.

completions.label: Full-bleed Layout

documentation.value: Creates a layout where content is centered but some elements can break out to full width.

completions.label: Glass Morphism

documentation.value: Creates a frosted glass effect using `backdrop-filter: blur()` with a semi-transparent background.

completions.label: Scroll Snap Carousel

documentation.value: Creates a horizontal carousel with mandatory scroll snap points.
Sets up a container query context and a responsive rule that responds to the container's width.

completions.label: Aspect Ratio Box

documentation.value: Creates a responsive container that maintains a fixed aspect ratio. Uses the modern `aspect-ratio` property.
